nodes.label: Attrition Victory
nodes.rationale: Scored by surviving units on the board (1/2/3 vp for infantry/cavalry/artillery); reserve units count nothing; ties go to the second player. Revised June 2026 from scoring defeated enemy units so board presence decides the standstill.

nodes.label: Headquarters Capture Victory
nodes.rationale: Attacking successfully into the opponent's HQ wins; a mutual-removal tie also counts as capture.

nodes.label: Game Victory (3 Map Cards)
nodes.rationale: First to collect 3 map cards wins; battle loser goes first in the next battle.

nodes.label: Combat Resolution
nodes.rationale: Compare attacker power vs defender power at the battle hex: attacker greater = defender removed & attacker advances; defender greater = attacker removed; tie = both removed.

nodes.label: Support
nodes.rationale: Adjacent allied units add support to the battle hex; trenches deny attacker support, rivers deny both sides' support.

nodes.label: Trench
nodes.rationale: Revised July 2026: covers two edges and only denies attacking support across them. Gives no defense, never blocks the attack itself, never hinders the defender; ownership irrelevant (a captured trench serves its new occupant). Previously gave +1 defense.

nodes.label: River
nodes.rationale: New July 2026: sits on one border, support never crosses it for either side; attacks and movement cross freely, Airdrop may land beyond, Naval Barrage cannot remove it.

nodes.label: Mountain Terrain
nodes.rationale: Grey terrain in the battle hex gives +1 defense when attacked across that edge.

nodes.label: Forest Terrain
nodes.rationale: Green terrain gives +1 attack when the attacker's hex borders the battle hex along a forest edge.

nodes.label: Infantry Unit
nodes.rationale: 1 attack / 1 defense / 1 support; 1 vp. The line unit.

nodes.label: Cavalry Unit
nodes.rationale: 3 attack / 0 defense / 0 support; 2 vp. The killer.

nodes.label: Artillery Unit
nodes.rationale: 0 attack / 0 defense / 2 support; 3 vp. The force multiplier.

nodes.label: Headquarters
nodes.rationale: Gives 1 support to all adjacent hexes, has no inherent defense; units can reposition or attack through it.

nodes.label: Turn Order
nodes.rationale: Draw to 4, play 1, resolve, remove the played card from the game, discard the rest; a card may always resolve as a simple attack or reposition.

nodes.label: Map Cards
nodes.rationale: 12-card map deck defining terrain layout and HQ start positions; the battle victor takes the card.

nodes.label: Reposition Action
nodes.rationale: Move a unit to an adjacent empty hex or swap two adjacent units; may move/swap through a headquarters.

nodes.label: Deploy / Build Action
nodes.rationale: Take a unit or building from the player mat and place it on the board.

nodes.label: Attack Action
nodes.rationale: Select a unit to attack an adjacent occupied tile; resolved via combat resolution.

nodes.label: Deploy Infantry (Starting Card)
nodes.rationale: Guaranteed in the opening hand; places one infantry adjacent to a controlled hex.

nodes.label: Airdrop (Card)
nodes.rationale: Places an infantry on any empty hex; never dealt into an opening hand (house rule).

nodes.label: Conscription (Card)
nodes.rationale: Places two infantry adjacent to controlled hexes in sequence.

nodes.label: Attack +1 (Card)
nodes.rationale: Order an attack with +1 support to the calculation.

nodes.label: Mass Assault (Card)
nodes.rationale: Order an attack, then order another attack.

nodes.label: Careful Maneuvers (Card)
nodes.rationale: Reposition a unit, then attack with -1 support.

nodes.label: Reckless Maneuvers (Card)
nodes.rationale: Order an attack, then reposition a unit.

nodes.label: Ordered Withdraw (Card)
nodes.rationale: Order an attack; on a tie the attacker lives but does not take the hex.

nodes.label: Naval Barrage (Card)
nodes.rationale: Remove a trench or forest from an adjacent controlled hex, then order an attack; cannot remove a river.

nodes.label: Forced March (Card)
nodes.rationale: Reposition up to three times in sequence.

nodes.label: deploy Step Type
nodes.rationale: Places a unit from reserve; anywhere:true allows any empty hex (Airdrop).

nodes.label: trench Step Type
nodes.rationale: Digs a trench on a controlled hex across two contiguous edges (July 2026 rules: denies attacker support only).

nodes.label: attack Step Type
nodes.rationale: Orders one attack; mod adjusts attacker total, tieSpare spares attacker on tie, noAdvance holds ground (Ordered Withdraw).

nodes.label: reposition Step Type
nodes.rationale: Move or swap one unit; 'up to N moves' = list the step N times (Forced March).

nodes.label: barrage Step Type
nodes.rationale: Removes any single trench or whole forest anywhere on the board (June 2026 ruling); mountains are safe.

nodes.label: House Rule (basic attack/reposition fallback)
nodes.rationale: Any card may be burned as a basic Attack or Reposition instead of its printed steps; a card with weak printed steps shows high Simple% in the card report.

nodes.label: CARD_KEEP Table (engine.js)
nodes.rationale: Per-id AI reluctance (1-9) to burn a card as a basic action; unlisted ids default 5; biases Simple% readings.

nodes.label: Card Report Metrics (Win%/Simple%/Skip%/1stSight%/AvgSeen)
nodes.rationale: balance.js output: Win% correlation, Simple% printed-action-not-worth-it, Skip% dead turns (~0), 1stSight%+AvgSeen overpowered watchlist.

nodes.label: North Star: Skill over Luck
nodes.rationale: A stronger player should win more; measured by balance.js matchup skill premium; target adjacent tiers >=60%, two tiers apart >=75%.

nodes.label: North Star: Decisive Games
nodes.rationale: Fights happen, kills matter, holding ground matters; metrics 0kill%, first-blood-won, control-tracks-win.

nodes.label: North Star: No Dead Turns
nodes.rationale: A player always feels they can act; metric is the Skip% column; target ~0, any card above 2% investigated.

nodes.label: North Star: Balanced Start
nodes.rationale: Neither seat nor colour wins before the battle starts; first mover line + per-map Red%/1st%; target 45-55%.

nodes.label: North Star: Tie-rule not deciding too much
nodes.rationale: Tie-goes-to-2nd should break ties, not decide games; baseline ~25% (biggest open lever); target <=15% (Bill hasn't ruled).
nodes.norm_label: north star: tie-rule not deciding too much

nodes.label: Skill Premium (matchup luck-o-meter)
nodes.rationale: balance.js matchup metric measuring how much a stronger AI tier beats a weaker one.

nodes.label: create-card Skill
nodes.rationale: Skill that proposes cards graded against the card rubric.

nodes.label: create-map Skill
nodes.rationale: Skill that proposes maps graded against the map rubric.

nodes.label: run-tournament Skill
nodes.rationale: Skill that runs an AI tournament and grades the meta against the rubrics.

nodes.label: Art Pipeline (id-based lookup + fallback)
nodes.rationale: Drop <card-id>.png into game/art/, optimize, and the game looks art up by card id (art/<id>.jpg then .png), quietly falling back to the text-only card face when nothing matches.

nodes.label: dev/optimize-art.ps1
nodes.rationale: Sweeps heavy originals into design-docs/art-originals/, trims transparent margins, and writes web-weight versions back into game/art/.

nodes.label: Steampunk Napoleonic Field-Journal Style
nodes.rationale: Aged parchment, ink, brass and copper, earthy browns/olives/creams, matte patina, no gloss/neon/modern UI; extends the card frame kit in Player Card Art direction drafts.

nodes.label: Priority 1 Unit Emblems
nodes.rationale: Ink-black silhouette emblems for infantry/cavalry/artillery/HQ that must read at 32px; tinted red/blue in-game (Priority-1 emblems were skipped since drawn glyphs read fine).

nodes.label: Priority 2 Card Illustrations
nodes.rationale: 13 landscape 5:3 painted-sketch scenes saved as <id>.png that fill the blank artwork window of the card fronts.

nodes.label: Priority 3 Table Dressing
nodes.rationale: Menu title plaque, felt table background, and parchment hex-map texture.

nodes.label: Shared Negative Prompt
nodes.rationale: Appended to everything: no photorealism, 3D, neon, sci-fi chrome, gloss, modern UI, watermark, text, drop shadow, perspective, cropped edges.

nodes.label: Global Card Constraints (print specs)
nodes.rationale: Portrait poker 2.5x3.5in (5:7), 1/8in bleed to 825x1125px at 300dpi, orthographic straight-on single card, plain background, strong negative prompt.

nodes.label: Steampunk Napoleonic Field-Journal Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Aged yellowed parchment with creases/ink stains/coffee rings, earthy tones with brass and copper accents, subtle filigree + light mechanical motifs.

nodes.label: Card Front Prompt
nodes.rationale: Top-to-bottom layout: artwork window (~62% height), title banner, subtext panel; blank frame only, no text or unit art.

nodes.label: Card Back Prompt
nodes.rationale: Dominant central emblem/seal with perfect 180-degree rotational symmetry so the back reads identically upside-down; no text or orientation markers.

nodes.norm_label: deck validation (16 copies, one starting card, airdrop rule)

nodes.label: War of Attrition — orientation for Claude
nodes.norm_label: war of attrition — orientation for claude

nodes.label: War of Attrition (board game, digital edition)
nodes.norm_label: war of attrition (board game, digital edition)

nodes.label: Map Editor (terrain paint + Board hexes carve)
nodes.norm_label: map editor (terrain paint + board hexes carve)

nodes.label: River terrain (support denial both ways)
nodes.rationale: V0 terrain-crossing revision added rivers as a two-way support blocker distinct from mountains; attacks/moves/Airdrop still cross, and it is not barrageable so a river plan survives Naval Barrage.

nodes.label: Trenches (attacker-support denial)
nodes.rationale: V0 terrain-crossing revision replaced the old +1 defense trench with support denial: attacker support may not cross a trenched border, attack itself always crosses, defender support unaffected, ownership irrelevant to the rules.

nodes.label: Attrition victory by surviving field units (fieldScore)
nodes.rationale: June 2026 revision: attrition scores surviving units on the board (inf1/cav2/art3), reserves and raw kills no longer score — a one-kill turtle should not beat a side that dominated the board.
nodes.norm_label: attrition victory by surviving field units (fieldscore)

nodes.label: Tie-goes-to-second-mover attrition rule
nodes.rationale: Tie in attrition goes to whoever moved second; still decides ~26% of battles and is called out as the biggest open balance lever for Bill.

nodes.label: Concession + advisory heuristic (concedeAdvised)
nodes.rationale: Advisory-only foregone-conclusion heuristic: field-score gap beyond ~3 VP/turn AND no unit can reach the enemy HQ in remaining turns; UI hints the human, AI auto-concedes, simBattle never concedes so balance stats stay full-game.

nodes.label: Anti-degeneracy AI guards
nodes.rationale: Three guards fixed degenerate AI play: -80 noopPenalty killed dead turns (Skip% 65-75%→0), the attrition projection killed the swap-dance stalemate (zero-kill 44%→3%, 2nd-player win 78%→56%), and anti-shuffle penalizes re-swapping the same pair.

nodes.label: No-op turn penalty + visible dead turns (Skip%)
nodes.rationale: Bill's principle: a player should always feel they can act, so dead turns must be loud and measured — pending.acted counts real actions and 0 logs a no-effect line and marks Skip%.
nodes.norm_label: no-op turn penalty + visible dead turns (skip%)

nodes.label: Flexible orders house rule (attack/reposition fallback)
nodes.rationale: Feedback Round 1: basic reposition refused while a basic attack is possible, to encourage attacking over shuffling.

nodes.label: Combat clarity QoL (hover attack pills, strike arrows, one-click trenches)
nodes.norm_label: combat clarity qol (hover attack pills, strike arrows, one-click trenches)

nodes.label: Physical terrain stock + single-hex piece rule
nodes.norm_label: physical terrain stock + single-hex piece rule
